D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Response Time & Overdrive

slow pixels smear a moving edge into a ghost; overdrive cures it — but push too far and the cure overshoots

A liquid-crystal cell takes time to twist, so a fast-moving edge leaves a fading trail — ghosting. Overdrive fights it by briefly driving the cell past its target voltage to flip it faster. Too little and the ghost remains; too much and the pixel overshoots, leaving a bright or dark inverse-ghost behind the edge.

A bar sweeps across. Raise the response time to lengthen the ghost trail, then add overdrive to sharpen it. Push overdrive past the sweet spot and an inverse-ghost (a bright/dark overshoot) appears at the trailing edge.
